text.title: code-quality-trends Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for code-quality-trends as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Treating security checklist items as binary tickboxes without threat modeling.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Real security requires understanding attack vectors, not just checking boxes; pair with security-review skill for sensitive changes.

failure-mode.description: Stale-trend hallucination: agent cites benchmarks not in the doc (training data leaking in).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use quote-only mode in prompt: "Use only the attached trend doc; do not cite from memory."

failure-mode.description: Generated configs reference non-existent lint rules.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always run the linter once on emit; auto-revert if it exits non-zero.

failure-mode.description: Coverage gates without test infrastructure produce false greens.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Verify test count > 0 before enforcing a coverage threshold; --cov-fail-under=80 with zero tests still passes.

failure-mode.description: Statistics parroting: agent quotes "81% of teams use AI" to justify a decision.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Filter statistical claims out of decision contexts; cite primary surveys when needed.
